assertion_results.text: A SKILL.md file exists in the output skill directory
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
assertion_results.evidence: Frontmatter contains name: Cleaning-DataFrames-with-Pandas and description: 'Use when cleaning, preprocessing, or standardizing tabular data...'

assertion_results.text: The description includes 'Use when' trigger conditions mentioning data cleaning or pandas
assertion_results.evidence: Description contains: 'Use when cleaning, preprocessing, or standardizing tabular data in Python with pandas.' Mentions both data cleaning and pandas.

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers handling missing values with code examples
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Handling Missing Values' (lines 56-99) with subsections 'Inspect first' and 'Strategy selection'. Code examples include df.dropna(subset=[...]), df['status'].fillna('unknown'), df['revenue'].fillna(df['revenue'].median()), df['score'].fillna(df['score'].mean()), df['price'].ffill(), df['temperature'].interpolate()

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers deduplication with code examples
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Deduplicating Rows' (lines 101-119) with code: df.duplicated().sum(), df.drop_duplicates(subset=['user_id', 'timestamp'], keep='first'), df.drop_duplicates(subset=['user_id'], keep='last')

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers column name normalization or type conversion
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Normalizing Column Names' (lines 121-142) with normalize_columns function using regex. Section '## Converting Types' (lines 143-165) with pd.to_datetime, pd.to_numeric, astype('category'), astype('Int64'), and boolean mapping.

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body uses progressive disclosure by referencing separate files for detailed patterns
assertion_results.evidence: The SKILL.md does not reference any separate files for detailed patterns. The references/cleaning-strategies.md file exists but is never referenced from SKILL.md. All content is inline in the main file.

assertion_results.text: An evals/evals.json file exists with at least 2 test cases
The SKILL.md body includes a Common mistakes or pitfalls section
assertion_results.evidence: Line 248: '## Common Mistakes' section with a table of 6 mistake/fix pairs covering modifying original DataFrame, errors='ignore', deduplication without subset, etc.

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body contains a workflow or ordered checklist for the cleaning pipeline
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Full Cleaning Pipeline' (lines 167-246) contains a numbered clean_dataframe function with ordered steps: 1. Normalize column names, 2. Drop rows missing required columns, 3. Deduplicate, 4. Convert types, 5. Fill nulls, 6. Reset index. The function itself serves as an ordered pipeline.
